mappings.rationale: Directly covers basic economic concepts including scarcity, division of labour, diminishing returns, and comparative advantage.
Directly covers microeconomic analysis of individual, household, and firm decision-making (subsection B.1).
Covers the private enterprise economy and Western-type market economies (subsections C.1.a and C.2).
Covers centrally planned economies and Soviet-type economic systems (subsections C.1.b and C.3).
Covers the socialist model of economic organization and mixed economies (subsections C.1.b and C.1.c).
Covers market-oriented economic systems and the debate between state and market roles in the economy.
Covers how actual economic behaviour departs from rational choice models, enriching basic economic concepts.
Covers strategic decision-making relevant to competition and comparative advantage (subsections A.6 and A.7).
Covers the theory of rational choice among scarce resources, directly relevant to subsection A.1.
Covers how different economic systems interact in the global economy, relevant to comparing systems (subsection C).
Covers Marxist economic theory including critiques of capitalism and theories of capital and labour.
Covers foundational economic concepts including division of labour, comparative advantage, and market competition.
Covers Keynesian macroeconomic concepts and the mixed economy model (subsections A.8 and C.1.c).
Covers economic development concepts and mixed economies in developing countries (subsections A.8 and C.4).
Covers the historical comparison of different economic systems across countries.
Covers mathematical tools used in economic analysis including optimization and marginality concepts.
Provides a case study of the transition from centrally planned to mixed economic systems.
Covers mixed economies in major developing countries, relevant to subsection C.4.
Covers the concept of division of labour and economic activity at the individual level.
Covers Mill's economic philosophy including competition, comparative advantage, and the mixed economy.
